steps.text: Start with the given equation $b - 19 = \dfrac{x}{y}$. To isolate $x$, multiply both sides by $y$: $y(b - 19) = x$.
Distribute $y$ on the left: $x = by - 19y$. This is the expression for $x$ in terms of $b$ and $y$.

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $x = \dfrac{b-19}{y}$ — divides by $y$ instead of multiplying; substituting gives $\dfrac{(b-19)/y}{y} = \dfrac{b-19}{y^2} \neq b-19$. Eliminated.
  • B: $x = by - 19y$ — equals $y(b-19)$; substituting gives $\dfrac{by-19y}{y} = b-19$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: $x = \dfrac{by-19}{y}$ — distributes incorrectly; this would give $b - \dfrac{19}{y}$ after substitution, not $b-19$. Eliminated.
  • D: $x = by - 19$ — fails to multiply the 19 by $y$; substituting gives $\dfrac{by-19}{y} = b - \dfrac{19}{y} \neq b-19$. Eliminated.
